title: X-tra Crispy Australian Vegetables Flatbread
description: A crowd-pleasing Australian vegetarian dish featuring fresh, crunchy vegetables, nuka-pickle, and the subtle flavor of orange blossom water.

ingredients.name: Flatbread (store-bought or homemade)
ingredients.notes: Look for a flatbread that is high in fiber and low in sodium.

ingredients.name: Nuka-pickle
ingredients.notes: Use store-bought nuka-pickle or make your own by fermentation.

ingredients.name: Orange blossom water
ingredients.notes: Adjust to taste for the subtle fragrance.

ingredients.name: Carrots
ingredients.notes: Choose a mix of colors for added visual appeal.

ingredients.name: Zucchini
ingredients.notes: Pat dry with a paper towel to remove excess moisture.

ingredients.name: Red bell pepper
ingredients.notes: Any color bell pepper will do.

ingredients.name: Olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality extra virgin olive oil.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use sea salt or Himalayan pink salt for added flavor.

instructions: Roll out the flatbread to a thickness of about 1/8 inch (3 mm).
Transfer the flatbread to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, trimming the edges to fit.
Mix the grated carrots, sliced zucchini, sliced red bell pepper, and chopped parsley in a bowl.
Brush the flatbread with 2 tablespoons of olive oil, leaving a 1-inch (2.5 cm) border around the edges.
Sprinkle the mixed vegetables over the flatbread, leaving a small border around the edges.
Drizzle the nuka-pickle over the vegetables, followed by the orange blossom water.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until the flatbread is crispy and golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
